content.text: Notching the cleanup.

params.intent: Step 2 follow-on cleanup of rbgp_depot_levy surfaced by post-edit review. (1) Auth/region consolidation: the 'Validate input parameters' wrapping step (renamed to 'Validate region against Artifact Registry locations' in the prior notch) authenticated once at line 578 to call the AR locations API for region validation, then the next step 'Authenticate as Payor' authenticated again at line 597 with a duplicate 'local z_token' declaration and identical zrbgp_authenticate_capture call — the first token was unused after the region check. Reordered: 'Authenticate as Payor' becomes step 1 with the single 'local z_token' capture, 'Validate region against Artifact Registry locations' becomes step 2 reusing that token. One auth, one token, dependency between the two steps now explicit (region-check requires authenticated AR access). (2) Dead RBRP env-var checks dropped: the three 'test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die ...' / RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID / RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID lines under the old 'Authenticate as Payor' step were belt-and-suspenders dead code — zrbrp_enforce in zrbgp_furnish already validates these are non-empty before any rbgp command runs (rbgp_cli.sh line 63), and Step 2 unconditionalizing zrbrr_enforce makes the analogous-pattern asymmetry stand out. Removed all three. (3) Joint-length validation added at the right layer: zrbrr_enforce (rbrr_regime.sh) gains a custom check after the CLOUD_PREFIX/RUNTIME_PREFIX format checks asserting $(( ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + 2 + ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} )) -le 30, with a buc_die message naming each component's length and the joint sum so an operator can immediately see which knob to dial back. The previous inline 30-char guard in levy fired only after auth + region API + project body construction; the regime-kindle layer catches the failure before any GCP API call. Worst-case math under existing individual caps: CLOUD_PREFIX max 11 (incl. trailing hyphen) + 'd-'(2) + MONIKER max 26 = 39 chars; the joint cap excludes the 30..39 sliver from regime validity. P2 #4 (RBAGS rename) withdrawn after rechecking AXLA-Lexicon.adoc:51 — RBAGS is an active umbrella xref ('Recipe Bottle Admin Google Specification'), used consistently across three buc_doc_brief sites (payor_install, depot_levy, payor_refresh); not stale, leaving as-is. Build green: rbtd-b, rbtd-t (58/58), rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast (97/97). Existing fast-suite negative tests don't probe the new joint-length check (the rbrr-bad-cloud-prefix-too-long case fires on individual-length cap before joint sum matters); a future case 'rbrr-joint-length-too-long' would round out coverage but adds a Rust fixture surface, deferred.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Regime Repo - Validator Module

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBRR_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrr multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBRR_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbrr_*)

zrbrr_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBRR_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrr already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all RBRR variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll RBRR

  buv_group_enroll "Resource Prefixing"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX         2   11  "Prefix prepended to cloud-visible resource names (GAR, Cloud Build, GCP)"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX       2   11  "Prefix prepended to local container and network names"

  buv_group_enroll "Vessel and Local Configuration"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_VESSEL_DIR           1  255  "Vessel definitions directory"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE     1   64  "Container workspace path for bottle working directory"
  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRR_DNS_SERVER                   "DNS server for containers"

  buv_group_enroll "GCP Infrastructure"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER            1   26  "Depot moniker — paired with CLOUD_PREFIX to derive depot project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket"
  buv_gname_enroll   RBRR_GCP_REGION               1   32  "GCP region"

  buv_group_enroll "Google Cloud Build Configuration"
  buv_gname_enroll   RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE           3   64  "Machine type for Cloud Build (CE format)"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT                2   10  "Build timeout (e.g., 1200s)"
  buv_decimal_enroll RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS  1  999  "Min concurrent builds required"

  buv_group_enroll "Secrets Directory"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_SECRETS_DIR              1  512  "Directory containing credential files"

  buv_group_enroll "Public Docs"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL          1  512  "Public docs URL — readme target for buh_tlt glossary links, updated per-release or per-incorporation"

  # Guard against unexpected RBRR_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel RBRR RBRR_

  # Build docker env args array from validated values
  # Usage: docker run "${ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV[@]}" ...
  ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV=("-e" "RBRR_DNS_SERVER=${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}")
  readonly ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV

  # Lock all enrolled RBRR_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock RBRR

  readonly ZRBRR_KINDLED=1
}

zrbrr_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBRR_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrr not kindled - call zrbrr_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all RBRR enrollment validations and custom format checks
zrbrr_enforce() {
  zrbrr_sentinel

  buv_vet RBRR

  buv_dir_exists "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}"
  buv_dir_exists "${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}"

  [[ "${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}" =~ ^[0-9]+s$ ]] \
    || buc_die "Invalid RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT format: ${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT} (expected NNNs)"

  [[ "${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ ]] \
    || buc_die "Invalid RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER format: ${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} (expected lowercase alphanumeric starting with letter; no hyphens)"

  [[ "${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}"   =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$ ]] \
    || buc_die "Invalid RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX format: ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}   (expected lowercase starting with letter, ending in hyphen)"

  [[ "${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$ ]] \
    || buc_die "Invalid RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX format: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX} (expected lowercase starting with letter, ending in hyphen)"

  # Joint-length cap: GCP project IDs max 30 chars. RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID
  # composes as "${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}".
  test $(( ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + 2 + ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} )) -le 30 \
    || buc_die "Joint length exceeds 30-char GCP project ID limit: RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX(${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}) + 'd-'(2) + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER(${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}) = $(( ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + 2 + ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} ))"

}

# eof

lines: # Joint-length cap: GCP project IDs max 30 chars. RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID

-    || buc_die "Joint length exceeds 30-char GCP project ID limit: RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX(${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}) + 'd-'(2) + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER(${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}) = $(( ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + 2 + ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} ))"
+    || buc_die "Joint length exceeds 30-char GCP project ID limit: RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX(${z_prefix_len}) + 'd-'(2) + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER(${z_moniker_len}) = ${z_joint_len}"

params.intent: DRY the joint-length check in zrbrr_enforce. Prior commit had ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} repeated 4 times, ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} repeated 4 times, and the (prefix + 2 + moniker) sum computed inline twice (once in the test, once in the buc_die message). Factored to three local -r bindings: z_prefix_len, z_moniker_len, z_joint_len. Test and message now reference each computed value once. The 'd-' literal still appears once in the arithmetic and once in the message body — different roles (calculation vs operator-facing explanation), kept both. Build green; rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast 97/97.

content.text: b7b418b1331c6fbb7109cafd69b5791e258fcddb

## Summary

Three commits landed this session on top of Step 1's `3c9d38fb`:

| Commit | Scope |
|--------|-------|
| `ee816373` | Step 2: `rbgp_depot_levy` reshape — zero-arg, RBDC-driven, banner trimmed, differential-furnish skip removed, fact-file constant + spec entry dropped |
| `4a66a4ce` | Post-review cleanup — auth/region consolidation (one token, one auth), dead RBRP env-var checks dropped, joint-length validation added to `zrbrr_enforce` |
| `b7b418b1` | DRY the joint-length check (3 `local -r` bindings replace 8 repeated `${#…}` substitutions and a duplicated arithmetic) |

All verifications green throughout: `rbtd-b`, `rbtd-t` 58/58, `rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast` 97/97, `vow-b`.

## Recommendation: don't wrap — proceed to Step 3

Original instructions explicitly said *"Do NOT close the pace — Step 3 (unmake reshape) is a follow-up; BBAAM stays open."* That still holds. The reasons not to wrap are concrete:

1. **`rbgp_depot_unmake` still takes a CLI positional arg** (line 827: `local -r z_depot_project_id="${1:-}"`). After Step 2, levy is zero-arg and unmake is the asymmetric outlier — a user-visible inconsistency between paired operations.
2. **Unmake's `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX`/`RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT` derivations and timestamp-suffix-strip logic** (lines 912–915) are now orphan-by-shape; the strip logic specifically expects `«prefix»-d-«name»-«timestamp»` but post-Step-2 there's no timestamp to strip.
3. **Step 3 closes the levy/unmake symmetry** the paddock locked in.

### Proposed Step 3 scope (for the next prompt)

- Drop `local -r z_depot_project_id="${1:-}"` and the buc_require interactive confirmation's `${z_depot_project_id}` usage; derive from `${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}` (post-zrbrr_enforce, available unconditionally now that the differential skip is gone).
- Drop the prefix-strip + timestamp-suffix arithmetic on `z_without_prefix`; `z_depot_name` (used for pool-stem reconstruction) becomes `${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`.
- Reshape `z_pool_stem` local in unmake to use `${RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM}` (mirroring Step 2's levy treatment).
- Update unmake's tabtarget — same caveat as `rbw-dL`: the standard `"${@}"` pattern stays, no asymmetry.
- Update the unmake `buc_doc_brief`/`buc_doc_param` (drop the param doc).

### Step 4 / Step 5 are still open

- **Step 4** — per-moniker fact-file shape (`<moniker>.depot` files via `rbgp_depot_list`). Step 1 added the `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` and state constants but the producer/consumer wiring is pending.
- **Step 5** — pristine-lifecycle Rust fixture (cases 2 & 3): set `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` before levy invocation, drop positional args, replace dropped fact-file read with derivation. Already annotated `STEP-5-PENDING` in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`.

Step 3 is the cleanest next prompt — mechanically scoped, mirrors Step 2's shape, and closes the levy/unmake asymmetry without depending on Steps 4/5.

817	rbgp_depot_unmake() {
818	  zrbgp_sentinel
819	
820	  local -r z_depot_project_id="${1:-}"
821	
822	  buc_doc_brief "DANGER: Permanently destroy an entire depot infrastructure"
823	  buc_doc_param "depot_project_id" "The depot project ID to destroy"
824	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
825	
826	  buc_step 'Safety confirmation required'
827	  test -n "${z_depot_project_id}" || buc_die "Depot project ID required as first argument (current derived: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID})"
828	
829	  buc_require "DANGER: Permanently destroy depot ${z_depot_project_id} and ALL resources" "${z_depot_project_id}"
830	
831	  buc_step 'Authenticate as Payor'
832	  test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID is not set"
833	  test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID is not set"
834	  
835	  local z_token
836	  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor via OAuth"
837	
838	  buc_step 'Validate target depot'
839	  test -n "${z_depot_project_id}" || buc_die "Depot project ID required as first argument"
840	
841	  local -r z_project_info_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}"
842	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_project_info_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_validate"
843	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Validate depot project" "depot_destroy_validate"
844	  
845	  local z_lifecycle_state
846	  z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_validate" '.state // "UNKNOWN"') || buc_die "Failed to parse project state"
847	  
848	  if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" != "ACTIVE"; then
849	    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
850	      buc_die "Project already marked for deletion"
851	    else
852	      buc_die "Project state is ${z_lifecycle_state} - can only destroy ACTIVE projects"
853	    fi
854	  fi
855	
856	  buc_step 'Check for and remove liens'
857	  local -r z_liens_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens?parent=projects%2F${z_depot_project_id}"
858	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_liens_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_liens_list"
859	  rbgu_http_require_ok "List liens" "depot_destroy_liens_list"
860

862	  z_lien_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_liens_list" '.liens // [] | length') || buc_die "Failed to parse liens response"
863	  
864	  if test "${z_lien_count}" -gt 0; then
865	    buc_log_args "Found ${z_lien_count} lien(s) - removing them"
866	    local z_lien_names
867	    z_lien_names=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_liens_list" '.liens[].name' | tr '\n' ' ') || buc_die "Failed to extract lien names"
868	    
869	    for z_lien_name in ${z_lien_names}; do

rbgp_depot_unmake() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_depot_project_id="${1:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "DANGER: Permanently destroy an entire depot infrastructure"
  buc_doc_param "depot_project_id" "The depot project ID to destroy"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step 'Safety confirmation required'
  test -n "${z_depot_project_id}" || buc_die "Depot project ID required as first argument (current derived: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID})"

  buc_require "DANGER: Permanently destroy depot ${z_depot_project_id} and ALL resources" "${z_depot_project_id}"

  buc_step 'Authenticate as Payor'
  test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID is not set"
  test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID is not set"
  
  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor via OAuth"

  buc_step 'Validate target depot'
  test -n "${z_depot_project_id}" || buc_die "Depot project ID required as first argument"

  local -r z_project_info_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_project_info_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_validate"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Validate depot project" "depot_destroy_validate"
  
  local z_lifecycle_state
  z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_validate" '.state // "UNKNOWN"') || buc_die "Failed to parse project state"
  
  if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" != "ACTIVE"; then
    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
      buc_die "Project already marked for deletion"
    else
      buc_die "Project state is ${z_lifecycle_state} - can only destroy ACTIVE projects"
    fi
  fi

  buc_step 'Check for and remove liens'
  local -r z_liens_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens?parent=projects%2F${z_depot_project_id}"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_liens_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_liens_list"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List liens" "depot_destroy_liens_list"
  
  local z_lien_count
  z_lien_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_liens_list" '.liens // [] | length') || buc_die "Failed to parse liens response"
  
  if test "${z_lien_count}" -gt 0; then
    buc_log_args "Found ${z_lien_count} lien(s) - removing them"
    local z_lien_names
    z_lien_names=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_liens_list" '.liens[].name' | tr '\n' ' ') || buc_die "Failed to extract lien names"
    
    for z_lien_name in ${z_lien_names}; do
      if test -n "${z_lien_name}"; then
        buc_log_args "Removing lien: ${z_lien_name}"
        local z_delete_lien_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens/${z_lien_name}"
        rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_delete_lien_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_lien_delete"
        rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete lien" "depot_destroy_lien_delete"
      fi
    done
  fi

  buc_step 'Unlink billing account (releases quota immediately)'
  local -r z_billing_unlink_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_billing_unlink.json"
  echo '{"billingAccountName":""}' > "${z_billing_unlink_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build billing unlink body"

  local -r z_billing_unlink_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING}${RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/billingInfo"
  rbgu_http_json "PUT" "${z_billing_unlink_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_billing_unlink" "${z_billing_unlink_body}"

  local z_billing_unlink_code
  z_billing_unlink_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_destroy_billing_unlink") || z_billing_unlink_code=""
  if test "${z_billing_unlink_code}" = "200"; then
    buc_log_args "Billing account unlinked - quota released"
  else
    buc_warn "Could not unlink billing (HTTP ${z_billing_unlink_code}) - proceeding with deletion anyway"
  fi

  # Extract depot name from project ID for resource naming
  local z_without_prefix="${z_depot_project_id#"${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"}"
  local z_name_len=${#z_without_prefix}
  local z_ts_suffix_len=$((1 + RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN))
  local -r z_depot_name="${z_without_prefix:0:$((z_name_len - z_ts_suffix_len))}"

  buc_step 'Delete dual worker pools (if exist)'
  local -r z_pool_stem="rbw-${z_depot_name}${RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX}"

  # Delete tether pool
  local -r z_tether_del_id="${z_pool_stem}${RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER}"
  local -r z_tether_del_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/locations/${RBRR_GCP_REGION}${RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS}/${z_tether_del_id}"
  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_tether_del_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_pool_tether"
  local z_tether_del_code
  z_tether_del_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_destroy_pool_tether") || z_tether_del_code=""
  case "${z_tether_del_code}" in
    200|204|404) buc_log_args "Tether pool ${z_tether_del_id} cleanup: HTTP ${z_tether_del_code}" ;;
    *) buc_warn "Tether pool cleanup failed: HTTP ${z_tether_del_code} — proceeding" ;;
  esac

  # Delete airgap pool
  local -r z_airgap_del_id="${z_pool_stem}${RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_AIRGAP}"
  local -r z_airgap_del_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/locations/${RBRR_GCP_REGION}${RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS}/${z_airgap_del_id}"
  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_airgap_del_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_pool_airgap"
  local z_airgap_del_code
  z_airgap_del_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_destroy_pool_airgap") || z_airgap_del_code=""
  case "${z_airgap_del_code}" in
    200|204|404) buc_log_args "Airgap pool ${z_airgap_del_id} cleanup: HTTP ${z_airgap_del_code}" ;;
    *) buc_warn "Airgap pool cleanup failed: HTTP ${z_airgap_del_code} — proceeding" ;;
  esac

  buc_step 'Initiate depot deletion'
  local -r z_delete_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}"
  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_delete_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_delete"
  
  local z_delete_response
  z_delete_response=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_destroy_delete") || buc_die "Failed to get deletion response code"
  
  if test "${z_delete_response}" = "200" || test "${z_delete_response}" = "204"; then
    buc_log_args "Project deletion initiated successfully"
  else
    local z_error_msg
    z_error_msg=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_delete" '.error.message // "Unknown error"') || z_error_msg="HTTP ${z_delete_response}"
    buc_die "Failed to initiate project deletion: ${z_error_msg}"
  fi

  buc_step 'Verify deletion state transition'
  local -r z_max_attempts=12
  local z_attempt=1
  local z_final_state
  
  while test "${z_attempt}" -le "${z_max_attempts}"; do
    sleep 5
    buc_log_args "Checking deletion state (attempt ${z_attempt}/${z_max_attempts})"
    
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_project_info_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_state_check"

    local z_state_check_code
    z_state_check_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_destroy_state_check") || z_state_check_code=""
    if test "${z_state_check_code}" = "200"; then
      z_final_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_state_check" '.state // "UNKNOWN"') || z_final_state="UNKNOWN"

      if test "${z_final_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
        break
      fi
    fi
    
    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))
  done
  
  if test "${z_final_state}" != "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
    buc_die "Failed to verify deletion state transition. Current state: ${z_final_state}"
  fi

  buc_step 'Update depot tracking'
  zrbgp_depot_list_update || buc_log_args "Warning: Failed to update depot tracking after deletion"

  # Success
  buc_success "Depot ${z_depot_project_id} successfully marked for deletion"
  buc_info "Project Status: DELETE_REQUESTED"
  buc_info "Billing: Unlinked (quota released immediately)"
  buc_info "Grace period: Up to 30 days before permanent removal"
  buc_info "All infrastructure (Mason SA, repository, bucket) will be automatically removed"
}

rbgp_depot_list() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "List all depot instances and their status"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step 'Authenticate as Payor'
  test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID is not set"

# Extract depot name from project ID for resource naming
  local z_without_prefix="${z_depot_project_id#"${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"}"
  local z_name_len=${#z_without_prefix}
  local z_ts_suffix_len=$((1 + RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN))
  local -r z_depot_name="${z_without_prefix:0:$((z_name_len - z_ts_suffix_len))}"

  buc_step 'Delete dual worker pools (if exist)'
  local -r z_pool_stem="rbw-${z_depot_name}${RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX}"

  # Delete tether pool
  local -r z_tether_del_id="${z_pool_stem}${RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER}"
  local -r z_tether_del_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/locations/${RBRR_GCP_REGION}${RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS}/${z_tether_del_id}"
  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_tether_del_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_pool_tether"
  local z_tether_del_code
  z_tether_del_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_destroy_pool_tether") || z_tether_del_code=""
  case "${z_tether_del_code}" in
    200|204|404) buc_log_args "Tether pool ${z_tether_del_id} cleanup: HTTP ${z_tether_del_code}" ;;
    *) buc_warn "Tether pool cleanup failed: HTTP ${z_tether_del_code} — proceeding" ;;
  esac

  # Delete airgap pool
  local -r z_airgap_del_id="${z_pool_stem}${RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_AIRGAP}"
  local -r z_airgap_del_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/locations/${RBRR_GCP_REGION}${RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS}/${z_airgap_del_id}"
  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_airgap_del_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_pool_airgap"
  local z_airgap_del_code

  case "${z_airgap_del_code}" in

    *) buc_warn "Airgap pool cleanup failed: HTTP ${z_airgap_del_code} — proceeding" ;;
  esac

  buc_step 'Initiate depot deletion'
  local -r z_delete_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}"

Step 3 coding done. Awaiting your test/notch direction.

## Step 3 diff summary

**1 file changed, 9 insertions, 27 deletions** in `rbgp_depot_unmake`:

- **Drop CLI positional arg + param doc.** `local -r z_depot_project_id="${1:-}"` and `buc_doc_param "depot_project_id" ...` removed.
- **Drop dead validation tests** (3 sites): the two `test -n "${z_depot_project_id}"` checks (one with the "current derived" hint that's no longer needed, one that was a duplicate at the start of `Validate target depot`) and the redundant RBRP env-var pair (`RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID`) — same belt-and-suspenders pattern killed in levy's cleanup; `zrbrp_enforce` already covers them.
- **`buc_require` interactive prompt** now uses `${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}` for both the warning text and the operator-must-type confirmation token.
- **All `${z_depot_project_id}` URL/body references → `${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}`** across project-info, liens, billing-unlink, project-delete URLs and the success banner (~7 sites).
- **Drop the prefix-strip + timestamp arithmetic block entirely** (the `z_without_prefix` + `z_name_len` + `z_ts_suffix_len` + `z_depot_name` reconstruction). Post-collapse there's nothing to strip.
- **Drop `local -r z_pool_stem` local; replace its 2 uses with `${RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM}`** (mirrors levy's treatment).

## Out of Step 3 scope (deliberately untouched)

- `zrbgp_depot_list_update` call (Step-4 territory per Step 1's note).
- `RBSDU-depot_unmake.adoc` operation spec (parallel to RBSDE staleness — separate pace).
- `tt/rbw-dU.PayorUnmakesDepot.sh` tabtarget (standard zero-arg `${@}` pattern; no asymmetry).

Tree dirty, single file modified, no tests run yet per your instruction. Ready for your call on test+notch.

Function now takes its target from kindled regime via ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}. (2) Dead validation triplet dropped: the 'test -n "${z_depot_project_id}" || buc_die "... (current derived: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID})"' under 'Safety confirmation required' (zero-arg can never be empty), the duplicate 'test -n "${z_depot_project_id}"' under 'Validate target depot' (same), and the RBRP env-var pair 'test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}"' / 'test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}"' under 'Authenticate as Payor' — same belt-and-suspenders dead pattern killed in levy's Step 2 cleanup; zrbrp_enforce in zrbgp_furnish covers all three RBRP fields before any rbgp command runs. diff --git a/Tools/buk/buf_fact.sh b/Tools/buk/buf_fact.sh
index 322a25c9..1ba3eb41 100644
--- a/Tools/buk/buf_fact.sh
+++ b/Tools/buk/buf_fact.sh
@@ -31,16 +31,38 @@ ZBUF_SOURCED=1
 # Tinder constants (pure string literals — available at source time)
 BUF_burx_env="burx.env"
 
+# Multi-fact extension registry (BUK-domain only). Constant value matches
+# constant name downcased; the buf_ext_ prefix in the value carries the
+# owning module identity so any fact file is recognizable on disk.
+BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias"
+
 # Write a named fact-file to both output and temp directories.
 # Dies if either copy already exists (double-write indicates a bug).
 # Args: <filename> <value>
-buf_write_fact() {
+buf_write_fact_single() {
   local -r z_filename="$1"
   local -r z_value="$2"
   local -r z_output_path="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_filename}"
   local -r z_temp_path="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${z_filename}"
-  test ! -f "${z_output_path}" || { echo "FATAL: buf_write_fact: preexists in output dir: ${z_output_path}" >&2; return 1; }
-  test ! -f "${z_temp_path}"   || { echo "FATAL: buf_write_fact: preexists in temp dir: ${z_temp_path}" >&2; return 1; }
+  test ! -f "${z_output_path}" || { echo "FATAL: buf_write_fact_single: preexists in output dir: ${z_output_path}" >&2; return 1; }
+  test ! -f "${z_temp_path}"   || { echo "FATAL: buf_write_fact_single: preexists in temp dir: ${z_temp_path}" >&2; return 1; }
+  printf '%s\n' "${z_value}" > "${z_output_path}"
+  printf '%s\n' "${z_value}" > "${z_temp_path}"
+}
+
+# Write a multi-element fact-file (<file_root>.<ext>) to both output and
+# temp directories. Dies if either copy already exists. Extension is an
+# opaque pass-through — no validation, no closed-set enforcement.
+# Args: <file_root> <ext> <value>
+buf_write_fact_multi() {
+  local -r z_root="$1"
+  local -r z_ext="$2"
+  local -r z_value="$3"
+  local -r z_filename="${z_root}.${z_ext}"
+  local -r z_output_path="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_filename}"
+  local -r z_temp_path="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${z_filename}"
+  test ! -f "${z_output_path}" || { echo "FATAL: buf_write_fact_multi: preexists in output dir: ${z_output_path}" >&2; return 1; }
+  test ! -f "${z_temp_path}"   || { echo "FATAL: buf_write_fact_multi: preexists in temp dir: ${z_temp_path}" >&2; return 1; }
   printf '%s\n' "${z_value}" > "${z_output_path}"
   printf '%s\n' "${z_value}" > "${z_temp_path}"
 }

content.text:
## Notch landed at `b010808d`

Step 3 committed cleanly — the explicit `files` list scoped the commit to `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh` only.

## Overlap with other ongoing work — yes, but no conflict

`jjx_record` warned about uncommitted changes in `Tools/buk/buf_fact.sh` that I never touched. Inspecting the working tree:

**Another officium is mid-flight on Step 4 (multi-fact infrastructure).** Their dirty edits to `buf_fact.sh`:
- Add `BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias"` tinder constant — first member of a multi-fact extension registry.
- Rename `buf_write_fact` → `buf_write_fact_single` (single-element fact files).
- Add new `buf_write_fact_multi` writing `<root>.<ext>` files — exactly the per-moniker `<moniker>.depot` shape Step 1 anticipated for Step 4.

This is the work the paddock locked in: "Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`)." Probably another `₣BB` officium working on `BBAA?` Step 4 in parallel.

### No content overlap with my Step 3
- My commit touched `rbgp_Payor.sh` (unmake function only).
- Their work touches `buf_fact.sh`.
- Different files, different concerns.
- Per CLAUDE.md additive-only discipline, I left their dirty file alone.

### One thing they need to be aware of (their pace, not mine)
Their `buf_write_fact → buf_write_fact_single` rename will break **22 existing call sites** I just enumerated (in `bud_dispatch.sh`, `butcbx_BurxExchange.sh`, `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` ×16, `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh`, `rbgp_Payor.sh:1179` — the last surviving Payor call after Step 2 dropped the depot_project_id one). They'll need either a thin compatibility wrapper or a sweep. Not my problem to fix; flagging because their commit will fail tests until that's resolved.

### Bottom line for Step 3
My Step 3 unmake reshape and their Step 4 fact-file work are non-overlapping in file scope. If they land first, their `buf_write_fact_single` rename touches `rbgp_Payor.sh:1179` (the surviving governor SA email call), but **not** the unmake function I just edited — so a clean rebase or merge will resolve without conflict. If I land first (already did), they merge their `buf_fact.sh` changes plus a sweep of remaining callers.

Tree currently shows the other officium's work still uncommitted. Their officium will eventually notch it.

content.text:
You're right — they handled it. My flag was a false alarm from inspecting only the one file `jjx_record` warned about.

## Verification of the other officium's work

**7 files dirty in their working tree, not just `buf_fact.sh`:**

| File | Their change | Status |
|------|-------------|--------|
| `Tools/buk/buf_fact.sh` | Rename `buf_write_fact` → `buf_write_fact_single`, add `buf_write_fact_multi`, add `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` tinder | Definition |
| `Tools/buk/bud_dispatch.sh` | `buf_write_fact` → `buf_write_fact_single` (1 call) | Swept ✓ |
| `Tools/buk/buts/butcbx_BurxExchange.sh` | All 3 sites swept + new `butcbx_multi_dual_write_tcase` exercising multi-write | Swept + extended ✓ |
| `Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` | All 16 sites swept | Swept ✓ |
| `Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` | 1 site swept | Swept ✓ |
| `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh` | Governor SA email call (line 1179) swept | Swept ✓ |
| `Tools/buk/burh_regime.sh` | New consumer: `burh_list_capture` emits `<alias>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}` observation facts (empty content, presence-is-fact pattern) | New consumer wired ✓ |

**Verification grep**: `grep -rn "buf_write_fact\b" --include="*.sh" | grep -v "_single\|_multi"` → zero results. No unswept callers anywhere.

## What it actually is

This isn't just Step 4 foundation — it's the **first real production consumer** of the multi-fact infrastructure. `burh_list_capture` is the BURH alias enumeration; their work makes alias profiles emit per-alias observation facts (`<alias>.buf_ext_alias`) so downstream tooling can inventory which BURH profiles are present. The `_single`/`_multi` rename disambiguates the two API shapes explicitly rather than overloading.

## Overlap with my Step 3

My Step 3 unmake reshape sits in `rbgp_Payor.sh` lines ~817–955 (the unmake function). Their dirty change in the same file is at line 1179 (governor_mantle). Different functions, no line overlap — when they notch, it'll be a clean overlay on my commit.

So: false alarm from me, their work is rigorously complete, and our two parallel paces will merge without conflict.

content.text:
Step 3 verification complete. All green across the board.

## Step 3 verification summary

| Tier | Result |
|------|--------|
| `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` | green |
| `tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh` | 58/58 unit tests pass |
| `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` | 97/97 (47 enrollment + 27 regime-validation + 8 regime-smoke + 15 handbook-render) |
| `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` | 28/28 across 5 fixtures (added for due diligence given parallel `buf_fact.sh` work) |

The BUK self-test surfaces interesting confirmation of the other officium's parallel work:

- `butcbx_burx_dual_write_tcase` / `butcbx_burx_preexist_tcase` — the original tests now exercising the renamed `buf_write_fact_single`. Pass.
- `butcbx_multi_dual_write_tcase` / `butcbx_multi_preexist_tcase` / `butcbx_multi_empty_content_tcase` — three new tests exercising the just-added `buf_write_fact_multi`. All pass.

So my Step 3 unmake reshape and the other officium's multi-fact infrastructure both pass under the same working tree, with no interaction issues. Their work is verified end-to-end (foundation + sweep + new consumer + new tests) even before they notch.

## Status

- Step 3 (`b010808d`) tested clean.
- Other officium has dirty working tree they will notch independently.
- ₢BBAAM stays open per your earlier instruction.

Steps remaining on `BBAAM` (per Step 1's roadmap):
- **Step 4** — per-moniker fact-file shape (`rbgp_depot_list` emitting `<moniker>.depot` files via `buf_write_fact_multi` with `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT`). The other officium has now laid the producer-side primitive (`buf_write_fact_multi`) and demonstrated the consumer pattern (`burh_list_capture` writing `<alias>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}`); their work makes Step 4 mechanically simpler.
- **Step 5** — pristine-lifecycle Rust fixture reshape (cases 2 & 3 set `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`, drop positional args, derive project_id from `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` rather than reading the dropped fact-file).

Step 4 is the natural next prompt — it consumes the foundation the other officium just built, and Step 5 needs Step 4's `rbgp_depot_list` reshape to settle the per-moniker fact-file shape before the Rust fixture can autodetect family-stem monikers cleanly.

Function now takes its target from kindled regime via ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}. (2) Dead validation triplet dropped: the 'test -n "${z_depot_project_id}" || buc_die "... (current derived: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID})"' under 'Safety confirmation required' (zero-arg can never be empty), the duplicate 'test -n "${z_depot_project_id}"' under 'Validate target depot' (same), and the RBRP env-var pair 'test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}"' / 'test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}"' under 'Authenticate as Payor' — same belt-and-suspenders dead pattern killed in levy's Step 2 cleanup; zrbrp_enforce in zrbgp_furnish covers all three RBRP fields before any rbgp command runs. (3) Interactive confirmation reshape: 'buc_require "DANGER: Permanently destroy depot ${z_depot_project_id} and ALL resources" "${z_depot_project_id}"' switched to use ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID} for both the warning banner and the operator-must-type confirmation token. (4) URL/body z_depot_project_id sweep: 7 sites in unmake (z_project_info_url, z_liens_url, z_billing_unlink_url, z_tether_del_url, z_airgap_del_url, z_delete_url, the buc_success final-banner string) all switched to ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}. (5) Prefix-strip + timestamp-suffix arithmetic block dropped entirely: the four-line block reconstructing z_depot_name via 'z_without_prefix="${z_depot_project_id#"${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"}"' + z_name_len + z_ts_suffix_len=$((1 + RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN)) + substring slice has no purpose post-collapse — there's no timestamp to strip and the moniker is available directly via RBRR. (6) z_pool_stem local dropped in favor of ${RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM} at both tether and airgap delete sites (mirrors levy's treatment in Step 2). Out-of-Step-3-scope (deliberately): zrbgp_depot_list_update call retained (rbgp_depot_list reshape lives in Step 4 territory per Step 1 commit's annotation); RBSDU-depot_unmake.adoc operation spec staleness parallel to RBSDE (separate pace material); tt/rbw-dU.PayorUnmakesDepot.sh tabtarget unchanged (standard zero-arg ${@} pattern, no asymmetry with peers). Not yet tested per user instruction — build/unit/fast-suite verification pending the next pass.
b7b418b1 jjb:1011-4a66a4ce:₢BBAAM:n: DRY the joint-length check in zrbrr_enforce. Prior commit had ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} repeated 4 times, ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} repeated 4 times, and the (prefix + 2 + moniker) sum computed inline twice (once in the test, once in the buc_die message). Factored to three local -r bindings: z_prefix_len, z_moniker_len, z_joint_len. Test and message now reference each computed value once. The 'd-' literal still appears once in the arithmetic and once in the message body — different roles (calculation vs operator-facing explanation), kept both. Build green; rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast 97/97.
4a66a4ce jjb:1011-ee816373:₢BBAAM:n: Step 2 follow-on cleanup of rbgp_depot_levy surfaced by post-edit review. (1) Auth/region consolidation: the 'Validate input parameters' wrapping step (renamed to 'Validate region against Artifact Registry locations' in the prior notch) authenticated once at line 578 to call the AR locations API for region validation, then the next step 'Authenticate as Payor' authenticated again at line 597 with a duplicate 'local z_token' declaration and identical zrbgp_authenticate_capture call — the first token was unused after the region check. Reordered: 'Authenticate as Payor' becomes step 1 with the single 'local z_token' capture, 'Validate region against Artifact Registry locations' becomes step 2 reusing that token. One auth, one token, dependency between the two steps now explicit (region-check requires authenticated AR access). (2) Dead RBRP env-var checks dropped: the three 'test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die ...' / RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID / RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID lines under the old 'Authenticate as Payor' step were belt-and-suspenders dead code — zrbrp_enforce in zrbgp_furnish already validates these are non-empty before any rbgp command runs (rbgp_cli.sh line 63), and Step 2 unconditionalizing zrbrr_enforce makes the analogous-pattern asymmetry stand out. Removed all three. (3) Joint-length validation added at the right layer: zrbrr_enforce (rbrr_regime.sh) gains a custom check after the CLOUD_PREFIX/RUNTIME_PREFIX format checks asserting $(( ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + 2 + ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} )) -le 30, with a buc_die message naming each component's length and the joint sum so an operator can immediately see which knob to dial back. The previous inline 30-char guard in levy fired only after auth + region API + project body construction; the regime-kindle layer catches the failure before any GCP API call. Worst-case math under existing individual caps: CLOUD_PREFIX max 11 (incl. trailing hyphen) + 'd-'(2) + MONIKER max 26 = 39 chars; the joint cap excludes the 30..39 sliver from regime validity. P2 #4 (RBAGS rename) withdrawn after rechecking AXLA-Lexicon.adoc:51 — RBAGS is an active umbrella xref ('Recipe Bottle Admin Google Specification'), used consistently across three buc_doc_brief sites (payor_install, depot_levy, payor_refresh); not stale, leaving as-is. Build green: rbtd-b, rbtd-t (58/58), rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast (97/97). Existing fast-suite negative tests don't probe the new joint-length check (the rbrr-bad-cloud-prefix-too-long case fires on individual-length cap before joint sum matters); a future case 'rbrr-joint-length-too-long' would round out coverage but adds a Rust fixture surface, deferred.

"${0##*/}" "${@}"' pattern matches every other zero-arg tabtarget (rbw-dl, rbw-dU); levy is now genuinely zero-arg, so the pattern is no longer asymmetric. (2) Dead RBRP env-var checks dropped: the three 'test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die ...' / RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID / RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID lines under the old 'Authenticate as Payor' step were belt-and-suspenders dead code — zrbrp_enforce in zrbgp_furnish already validates these are non-empty before any rbgp command runs (rbgp_cli.sh line 63), and Step 2 unconditionalizing zrbrr_enforce makes the analogous-pattern asymmetry stand out. Removed all three. (3) Joint-length validation added at the right layer: zrbrr_enforce (rbrr_regime.sh) gains a custom check after the CLOUD_PREFIX/RUNTIME_PREFIX format checks asserting $(( ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + 2 + ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} )) -le 30, with a buc_die message naming each component's length and the joint sum so an operator can immediately see which knob to dial back. The previous inline 30-char guard in levy fired only after auth + region API + project body construction; the regime-kindle layer catches the failure before any GCP API call. Worst-case math under existing individual caps: CLOUD_PREFIX max 11 (incl. trailing hyphen) + 'd-'(2) + MONIKER max 26 = 39 chars; the joint cap excludes the 30..39 sliver from regime validity. P2 #4 (RBAGS rename) withdrawn after rechecking AXLA-Lexicon.adoc:51 — RBAGS is an active umbrella xref ('Recipe Bottle Admin Google Specification'), used consistently across three buc_doc_brief sites (payor_install, depot_levy, payor_refresh); not stale, leaving as-is. Build green: rbtd-b, rbtd-t (58/58), rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast (97/97). Existing fast-suite negative tests don't probe the new joint-length check (the rbrr-bad-cloud-prefix-too-long case fires on individual-length cap before joint sum matters); a future case 'rbrr-joint-length-too-long' would round out coverage but adds a Rust fixture surface, deferred.

Levy drops the CLI positional arg (z_depot_name capture, regex/length validation block, RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX-anchored param doc) and reads RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER from the kindled regime. Drops the timestamp generation block (date scratch-file dance, ${PREFIX}-d-${NAME}-${TIMESTAMP} project-id construction, 30-char length guard, buf_write_fact RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID). All inline derivations replaced with RBDC kindle constants: ${z_depot_project_id} -> ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID} (12 sites), z_pool_stem local dropped in favor of ${RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM}, z_build_bucket local dropped in favor of ${RBDC_GCS_BUCKET}, z_repository_name local dropped in favor of ${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}; z_mason_name retained as ${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} per paddock's Mason-SA-name-unchanged lock. content.text:
Tree is already clean — `b010808d` (Step 3) was notched before testing, and tests don't dirty the tree. Nothing of mine to notch right now. The other officium notched their parallel work at `bffc58cb` as `₢BBAAO` (a different pace under heat ₣BB) — confirms my earlier guess.

## Questions before Step 4

A few worth resolving before I start coding:

1. **Step 4 scope — producer only, or producer + state determination?** The paddock specifies file content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED` (tinder constants already landed in Step 1). Determining `BROKEN` requires post-levy resource-existence probing (does the GAR repo exist? the bucket? the pools?). Is Step 4 *just* the emit-per-moniker reshape (using whatever state-discovery already exists in `rbgp_depot_list`), or does it also include refining the state-discovery logic? I'd default to "just the reshape" — keep state discovery as today's prefix-scan + lifecycle-state read; Step 4 changes only the *output shape* to per-moniker `.depot` files.

2. **Old output format — replace or augment?** Current `rbgp_depot_list` emits a tabular stdout listing for operator consumption. Step 4's per-moniker fact files are a machine-readable *additional* surface (Rust pristine fixture will consume them). Should the operator-facing tabular stdout stay (additive: tabular for humans + per-moniker files for machines), or get replaced by something derived from the same fact-file emission? My read of the paddock is *additive* — payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space, fact files are observation-style.

3. **`zrbgp_depot_list_update` callers (levy and unmake) — revisit?** Both call it as a "refresh tracking" step at end. With Step 4's shape change, that update probably re-emits the per-moniker fact files. Anything to flag, or treat the call sites as untouched and let the function shape change ripple through naturally?

4. **The BBAAO side-effect they noted in `burh_list_capture`** — every call now emits an observation fact, with `burh_ssh_config` also calling capture. If both run in one dispatch and write the same `<alias>.buf_ext_alias` file, the dual-write preexist guard would fire. Their tests passed so apparently safe at present, but worth knowing whether Step 4's `rbgp_depot_list` will be called twice in any single dispatch (e.g., levy then unmake within the same fixture) — that would break the same way. Probably fine since each tabtarget is one dispatch, but flagging.

If "just the reshape, additive output, treat callers as untouched" is the answer to #1–#3 and #4 is a non-issue at the dispatch grain we operate at, I have what I need to start Step 4 cleanly.

buc_info "Grace period: Up to 30 days before permanent removal"
  buc_info "All infrastructure (Mason SA, repository, bucket) will be automatically removed"
}

rbgp_depot_list() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "List all depot instances and their status"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step 'Authenticate as Payor'
  test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID is not set"
  test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}"  || buc_die "RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID is not set"
  
  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor via OAuth"

  buc_step 'Query depot projects'
  local -r z_filter="projectId:${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-* AND lifecycleState:ACTIVE"
  local -r z_list_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects?filter=${z_filter// /%20}"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_list_projects"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List depot projects" "depot_list_projects"
  
  local z_project_count
  z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_list_projects" '.projects // [] | length') || z_project_count=0

  if test "${z_project_count}" -eq 0; then
    buc_info "No active depot projects found"
    return 0
  fi

  buc_step "Validating ${z_project_count} depot(s)"
  
  local z_depot_index=0
  local z_complete_count=0
  local z_broken_count=0
  
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "=== DEPOT SUMMARY ==="
  printf "%-40s %s\n" "PROJECT_ID" "STATUS"

  while test "${z_depot_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
    local z_project_id
    z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_list_projects" ".projects[${z_depot_index}].projectId") || continue

    # Strip this installation's depot prefix (CLOUD_PREFIX + 'd-') to recover
    # the moniker; projects outside the namespace are listed but skipped here
    # since their moniker can't be inferred from the new naming scheme.
    local z_depot_name=""
    if [[ "${z_project_id}" == "${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"* ]]; then
      z_depot_name="${z_project_id#"${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"}"
    fi

    local z_status="CHECKING"
    local z_mason_expected="${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-${z_depot_name}"

    local z_mason_email
    z_mason_email=$(rbgu_sa_email_capture "${z_mason_expected}" "${z_project_id}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to compose Mason email for depot ${z_depot_name}"
    local z_mason_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_email}"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_mason_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_list_mason_${z_depot_index}" || true

    local z_mason_code
    z_mason_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_list_mason_${z_depot_index}") || z_mason_code=""
    if test "${z_mason_code}" = "200"; then
      z_status="COMPLETE"
      z_complete_count=$((z_complete_count + 1))
    else
      z_status="BROKEN"
      z_broken_count=$((z_broken_count + 1))
    fi

    printf "%-40s %s\n" "${z_project_id}" "${z_status}"

    z_depot_index=$((z_depot_index + 1))
  done
  
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "=== SUMMARY ==="
  buc_info "Total depots: ${z_project_count}"
  buc_info "Complete: ${z_complete_count}"
  buc_info "Broken: ${z_broken_count}"
  
  if test "${z_broken_count}" -gt 0; then
    buc_info ""
    buc_info "Note: BROKEN depots may have missing resources (Mason SA, repository, or bucket)"
    buc_info "Consider investigating broken depots or destroying them if no longer needed"
  fi
}

rbgp_payor_oauth_refresh() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Refresh expired OAuth credentials following RBAGS manual procedure"
  buc_doc_lines "Use this when OAuth tokens expire after 6 months or are compromised"
  buc_doc_lines "Requires downloading new OAuth JSON from Google Cloud Console"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step 'Display OAuth refresh procedure'

  buc_info "OAuth credentials need to be refreshed. Follow these steps:"
  buc_info ""

  buc_info "   Console URL: https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials?project=${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID}"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "2. Find existing 'Recipe Bottle Payor' OAuth client"

zrbgp_depot_list_update() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_log_args "Updating depot project tracking in RBRP configuration"

  # Get OAuth access token
  local z_access_token
  z_access_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate for depot list update"

  # Query active depot projects (CRM v1 allows listing accessible projects without parent)
  # Using v1 instead of v3 which requires parent parameter
  local -r z_list_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_access_token}" "depot_list_tracking"

  # Non-blocking: if query fails, just log and continue (normal on first install with no projects)
  if ! rbgu_http_require_ok "Query depot projects" "depot_list_tracking" 2>/dev/null; then
    buc_log_args "Depot project list query skipped (expected on first install or API access restrictions)"
    return 0
  fi
  
  # Extract and validate depot project IDs
  local z_depot_ids=""
  local z_project_count
  z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_list_tracking" '.projects // [] | length') || z_project_count=0
  
  if test "${z_project_count}" -gt 0; then
    local z_index=0
    while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
      local z_project_id
      z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_list_tracking" ".projects[${z_index}].projectId") || continue
      
      # Match this installation's depot prefix (CLOUD_PREFIX + 'd-')
      if [[ "${z_project_id}" == "${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"* ]]; then
        z_depot_ids="${z_depot_ids} ${z_project_id}"
      else
        buc_log_args "Skipping project outside this installation's depot namespace: ${z_project_id}"
      fi
      
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
    done
  fi
  
  # Update RBRP configuration
  # Note: This would normally update rbrp.env file
  # For now, we'll export to environment and log the result
  buc_log_args "Found ${z_project_count} depot projects, ${z_depot_ids:+ $(($(printf '%s' "${z_depot_ids}" | wc -w))):+0} valid"

  return 0
}

content.text:
Apologies for the volume — let me close them tightly.

## 1) State scope — your answer fits

You're right; "we can check after deleting depot for that other state" is the answer. Today's `rbgp_depot_list` already classifies COMPLETE/BROKEN via Mason-SA HEAD probe (line 1014–1027). To emit DELETE_REQUESTED I just drop the `AND lifecycleState:ACTIVE` clause from the filter (line 975) and read `.lifecycleState` per project — DELETE_REQUESTED state → that file content; ACTIVE + Mason exists → COMPLETE; ACTIVE + Mason absent → BROKEN. Test sequence becomes natural: `levy` → list shows COMPLETE; `unmake` → list shows DELETE_REQUESTED. BROKEN remains opportunistic (only naturally observed when something fails mid-way).

Also need to update the project-id filter pattern: line 975 still uses old `${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-*` with the dash-between separator; post-collapse it should be `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-*` to match the new shape.

## 2) Stdout: no change in scope. Got it.

## 3) `zrbgp_depot_list_update` recommendation

Read it (line 143–191). It's effectively dead infrastructure today — it queries CRM v1, filters by prefix, and logs counts. Comment at line 187 says it out loud: *"This would normally update rbrp.env file. For now, we'll export to environment and log the result."* So it does nothing tracked.

**Recommendation:** factor the depot-state probe into one internal helper (e.g., `zrbgp_depot_state_emit`) that does the API call + state classification + per-moniker fact-file emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`. Both `rbgp_depot_list` (public, formats stdout afterward) and `zrbgp_depot_list_update` (called from levy/unmake postscripts) call this helper. `zrbgp_depot_list_update` becomes a thin wrapper that just runs the helper for its tracking-refresh side effect — finally living up to its name. The two callsites (line 810 in levy, line 951 in unmake) stay unchanged in their call signatures.

This keeps the existing call topology intact, makes "update tracking" actually update something (the fact files), and avoids duplicated probe code between the public list and the private update.

## 4) The BBAAO side-effect, expanded

The concrete contract: `buf_write_fact_multi` *dies* if either the output or temp copy of `<root>.<ext>` already exists. It's a single-shot-per-dispatch write.

`burh_list_capture` now calls `buf_write_fact_multi "${z_alias}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""` for *each* alias it enumerates. Their commit msg notes "side effect on burh_ssh_config which also calls capture." That's the BBAAO author flagging that two distinct CLI commands (`burh_list` and `burh_ssh_config`) both invoke `burh_list_capture`. Each runs in its own dispatch with a fresh `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` / `BURD_TEMP_DIR`, so within one dispatch the helper is called once and no collision happens.

**Why I flagged it for Step 4**: the same pattern applies to my work. If I have `zrbgp_depot_list_update` emit per-moniker fact files (per #3), and a single dispatch ends up calling that helper *twice* — for example, if levy probes the state early to check "does this moniker already exist?" and then calls `zrbgp_depot_list_update` again at the end — the second call's first per-moniker write would hit a die. The fix is the design rule: **emit fact files exactly once per dispatch.** In practice this just means I don't probe early; today's levy/unmake code already calls `zrbgp_depot_list_update` exactly once at the end, so following the same shape keeps it safe.

A latent concern, separate from Step 4 but worth knowing: `buf_write_fact_multi`'s die-on-preexist is a "writes are single-shot" API, not idempotent. If a future use case wants observation-style facts that can be re-asserted (write-if-not-exists), they'd need either a sibling `buf_assert_fact_multi` or a flag. Not my problem to fix now; flagging because it's a constraint I have to design around.

---

I'm ready to start Step 4. Plan: drop the `lifecycleState:ACTIVE` filter clause + update project-id pattern in `rbgp_depot_list`, factor a shared `zrbgp_depot_state_emit` helper that probes/classifies/emits fact files, leave both `rbgp_depot_list` (public+stdout) and `zrbgp_depot_list_update` (postscript) calling it. Stdout banner unchanged. No coupling to fixture identity (paddock lock).

zrbgp_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGP_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgp already kindled"

  buc_log_args "Ensure dependencies are kindled first"
  zrbgc_sentinel
  zrbgo_sentinel
  zrbgu_sentinel
  zrbgi_sentinel

  readonly ZRBGP_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_"
  readonly ZRBGP_EMPTY_JSON="${ZRBGP_PREFIX}empty.json"
  printf '{}' > "${ZRBGP_EMPTY_JSON}"

  readonly ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_scratch.txt"

  # Infix values for HTTP operations
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_DELETE="project_delete"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_RESTORE="project_restore"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE="project_state"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS="list_liens"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_DELETE_LIEN="delete_lien"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_ATTACH="billing_attach"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_DETACH="billing_detach"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_CREATE_REPO="create_repo"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_VERIFY_REPO="verify_repo"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_INFO="project_info"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE="bucket_create"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_API_CHECK="api_checking"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_LIST_SA="gov_list_sa"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_DELETE_SA="gov_delete_sa"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_CREATE_SA="gov_create_sa"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_VERIFY_SA="gov_verify_sa"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_KEY="gov_key"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_IAM="gov_iam"

  readonly ZRBGP_KINDLED=1
}

zrbgp_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBGP_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgp not kindled - call zrbgp_kindle first"
}

  buc_log_args "Exchanging refresh token for access token"

  # Build request and pipe to curl - secrets never touch disk
  local z_response
  z_response=$(jq -n \
    --arg refresh_token "${RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN}" \
    --arg client_id "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}" \
    --arg client_secret "${RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET}" \
    --arg grant_type "refresh_token" \
    '{
      refresh_token: $refresh_token,
      client_id: $client_id,

      grant_type: $grant_type

      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \

      -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
      -d @- \
      "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token") || buc_die "Failed to execute OAuth refresh request"

  # Check for error in response

content.text: Now replace `zrbgp_depot_list_update` with the new helper + simplified update wrapper.

input.old_string:
zrbgp_depot_list_update() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_log_args "Updating depot project tracking in RBRP configuration"

  # Get OAuth access token
  local z_access_token
  z_access_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate for depot list update"

  # Query active depot projects (CRM v1 allows listing accessible projects without parent)
  # Using v1 instead of v3 which requires parent parameter
  local -r z_list_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_access_token}" "depot_list_tracking"

  # Non-blocking: if query fails, just log and continue (normal on first install with no projects)
  if ! rbgu_http_require_ok "Query depot projects" "depot_list_tracking" 2>/dev/null; then
    buc_log_args "Depot project list query skipped (expected on first install or API access restrictions)"
    return 0
  fi
  
  # Extract and validate depot project IDs
  local z_depot_ids=""
  local z_project_count
  z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_list_tracking" '.projects // [] | length') || z_project_count=0
  
  if test "${z_project_count}" -gt 0; then
    local z_index=0
    while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
      local z_project_id
      z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_list_tracking" ".projects[${z_index}].projectId") || continue
      
      # Match this installation's depot prefix (CLOUD_PREFIX + 'd-')
      if [[ "${z_project_id}" == "${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"* ]]; then
        z_depot_ids="${z_depot_ids} ${z_project_id}"
      else
        buc_log_args "Skipping project outside this installation's depot namespace: ${z_project_id}"
      fi
      
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
    done
  fi
  
  # Update RBRP configuration
  # Note: This would normally update rbrp.env file
  # For now, we'll export to environment and log the result
  buc_log_args "Found ${z_project_count} depot projects, ${z_depot_ids:+ $(($(printf '%s' "${z_depot_ids}" | wc -w))):+0} valid"

  return 0
}
input.new_string:
# Probe per-installation depot states and emit per-moniker fact files.
# Each ACTIVE depot's Mason SA presence distinguishes COMPLETE from BROKEN;
# DELETE_REQUESTED projects are recorded as such. Per-moniker fact files
# named "<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" are written via buf_write_fact_multi
# with state content drawn from RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_*. A pipe-separated summary
# line "<moniker>|<state>|<project_id>" is appended to ZRBGP_DEPOT_STATE_SUMMARY
# for callers wanting to render operator-facing output without re-walking
# the API response.
#
# Single-shot per dispatch — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist, so a
# second call within the same dispatch would fatal at the first repeated
# moniker. By design: one tabtarget = one dispatch = one emit call.
#
# Args: <oauth_access_token>
zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbgp_depot_state_emit: token required"

  : > "${ZRBGP_DEPOT_STATE_SUMMARY}" || buc_die "Failed to truncate depot state summary"

  # No lifecycleState filter — DELETE_REQUESTED projects must surface so
  # their per-moniker fact files emit alongside ACTIVE ones.
  local -r z_prefix_match="${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-"
  local -r z_filter="projectId:${z_prefix_match}*"
  local -r z_list_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects?filter=${z_filter// /%20}"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_state_list"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List depot projects" "depot_state_list"

  local z_project_count
  z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_list" '.projects // [] | length') || z_project_count=0

  test "${z_project_count}" -gt 0 || return 0

  local z_index=0
  while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
    local z_project_id
    z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_list" ".projects[${z_index}].projectId") \
      || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); continue; }

    if [[ "${z_project_id}" != "${z_prefix_match}"* ]]; then
      buc_log_args "Skipping project outside this installation's depot namespace: ${z_project_id}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi
    local z_moniker="${z_project_id#"${z_prefix_match}"}"

    local z_lifecycle_state
    z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_list" ".projects[${z_index}].lifecycleState // \"UNKNOWN\"") \
      || z_lifecycle_state="UNKNOWN"

    local z_state=""
    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
      z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}"
    elif test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE"; then
      local z_mason_email
      z_mason_email=$(rbgu_sa_email_capture "${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-${z_moniker}" "${z_project_id}") \
        || buc_die "Failed to compose Mason email for moniker ${z_moniker}"
      local z_mason_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_email}"
      local z_mason_infix="depot_state_mason_${z_index}"
      rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_mason_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_mason_infix}" || true

      local z_mason_code
      z_mason_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_mason_infix}") || z_mason_code=""
      if test "${z_mason_code}" = "200"; then
        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}"
      else
        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN}"
      fi
    else
      buc_log_args "Skipping moniker ${z_moniker} with unrecognized lifecycle state: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi

    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" "${z_state}"
    printf '%s|%s|%s\n' "${z_moniker}" "${z_state}" "${z_project_id}" >> "${ZRBGP_DEPOT_STATE_SUMMARY}"

    z_index=$((z_index + 1))
  done
}

# Post-lifecycle hook: refresh per-moniker depot fact files.
# Called by levy and unmake at end-of-operation so observers (theurge
# autodetect, manual inspection) see fresh state.
zrbgp_depot_list_update() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_log_args "Refreshing per-moniker depot fact files"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate for depot fact refresh"

  zrbgp_depot_state_emit "${z_token}"
}

toolUseResult.oldString:
zrbgp_depot_list_update() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_log_args "Updating depot project tracking in RBRP configuration"

  # Get OAuth access token
  local z_access_token
  z_access_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate for depot list update"

  # Query active depot projects (CRM v1 allows listing accessible projects without parent)
  # Using v1 instead of v3 which requires parent parameter
  local -r z_list_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_access_token}" "depot_list_tracking"

  # Non-blocking: if query fails, just log and continue (normal on first install with no projects)
  if ! rbgu_http_require_ok "Query depot projects" "depot_list_tracking" 2>/dev/null; then
    buc_log_args "Depot project list query skipped (expected on first install or API access restrictions)"
    return 0
  fi
  
  # Extract and validate depot project IDs
  local z_depot_ids=""
  local z_project_count
  z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_list_tracking" '.projects // [] | length') || z_project_count=0
  
  if test "${z_project_count}" -gt 0; then
    local z_index=0
    while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
      local z_project_id
      z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_list_tracking" ".projects[${z_index}].projectId") || continue
      
      # Match this installation's depot prefix (CLOUD_PREFIX + 'd-')
      if [[ "${z_project_id}" == "${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"* ]]; then
        z_depot_ids="${z_depot_ids} ${z_project_id}"
      else
        buc_log_args "Skipping project outside this installation's depot namespace: ${z_project_id}"
      fi
      
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
    done
  fi
  
  # Update RBRP configuration
  # Note: This would normally update rbrp.env file
  # For now, we'll export to environment and log the result
  buc_log_args "Found ${z_project_count} depot projects, ${z_depot_ids:+ $(($(printf '%s' "${z_depot_ids}" | wc -w))):+0} valid"

  return 0
}

content.text:
Fair pushback. The summary file is unnecessary — the fact files emitted by `buf_write_fact_multi` *are* the data structure. They're already on disk in `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` keyed by moniker, with the state as content. Project_id is deterministic from moniker (`${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${moniker}`). `rbgp_depot_list` can walk the fact files directly to render stdout. No third intermediate needed.

Refactoring to drop the summary file.

  readonly ZRBGP_DEPOT_STATE_SUMMARY="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_depot_state_summary.txt"

  rbgu_http_require_ok "List depot projects" "depot_state_list"

  local z_project_count
  z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_list" '.projects // [] | length') || z_project_count=0

  test "${z_project_count}" -gt 0 || return 0

  local z_index=0
  while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
    local z_project_id
    z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_list" ".projects[${z_index}].projectId") \
      || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); continue; }

    if [[ "${z_project_id}" != "${z_prefix_match}"* ]]; then
      buc_log_args "Skipping project outside this installation's depot namespace: ${z_project_id}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi
    local z_moniker="${z_project_id#"${z_prefix_match}"}"

    local z_lifecycle_state
    z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_list" ".projects[${z_index}].lifecycleState // \"UNKNOWN\"") \
      || z_lifecycle_state="UNKNOWN"

    local z_state=""
    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
      z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}"
    elif test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE"; then
      local z_mason_email
      z_mason_email=$(rbgu_sa_email_capture "${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-${z_moniker}" "${z_project_id}") \
        || buc_die "Failed to compose Mason email for moniker ${z_moniker}"
      local z_mason_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_email}"
      local z_mason_infix="depot_state_mason_${z_index}"
      rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_mason_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_mason_infix}" || true

      local z_mason_code
      z_mason_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_mason_infix}") || z_mason_code=""
      if test "${z_mason_code}" = "200"; then
        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}"
      else
        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN}"
      fi
    else
      buc_log_args "Skipping moniker ${z_moniker} with unrecognized lifecycle state: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi

    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" "${z_state}"
    printf '%s|%s|%s\n' "${z_moniker}" "${z_state}" "${z_project_id}" >> "${ZRBGP_DEPOT_STATE_SUMMARY}"

    z_index=$((z_index + 1))
  done
}

# Probe per-installation depot states and emit per-moniker fact files.
# Each ACTIVE depot's Mason SA presence distinguishes COMPLETE from BROKEN;
# DELETE_REQUESTED projects are recorded as such. Per-moniker fact files
# named "<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" are written via buf_write_fact_multi
# with state content drawn from RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_*; consumers walk the
# emitted files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR/BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem is the data bus).
#
# Single-shot per dispatch — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist, so a
# second call within the same dispatch would fatal at the first repeated
# moniker. By design: one tabtarget = one dispatch = one emit call.
#
# Args: <oauth_access_token>
zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbgp_depot_state_emit: token required"

  # No lifecycleState filter — DELETE_REQUESTED projects must surface so
  # their per-moniker fact files emit alongside ACTIVE ones.
  local -r z_prefix_match="${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-"
  local -r z_filter="projectId:${z_prefix_match}*"
  local -r z_list_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects?filter=${z_filter// /%20}"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_state_list"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List depot projects" "depot_state_list"

  local z_project_count
  z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_list" '.projects // [] | length') || z_project_count=0

  test "${z_project_count}" -gt 0 || return 0

  local z_index=0
  while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
    local z_project_id
    z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_list" ".projects[${z_index}].projectId") \
      || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); continue; }

    if [[ "${z_project_id}" != "${z_prefix_match}"* ]]; then
      buc_log_args "Skipping project outside this installation's depot namespace: ${z_project_id}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi
    local z_moniker="${z_project_id#"${z_prefix_match}"}"

    local z_lifecycle_state
    z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_list" ".projects[${z_index}].lifecycleState // \"UNKNOWN\"") \
      || z_lifecycle_state="UNKNOWN"

    local z_state=""
    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
      z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}"
    elif test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE"; then
      local z_mason_email
      z_mason_email=$(rbgu_sa_email_capture "${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-${z_moniker}" "${z_project_id}") \
        || buc_die "Failed to compose Mason email for moniker ${z_moniker}"
      local z_mason_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_email}"
      local z_mason_infix="depot_state_mason_${z_index}"
      rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_mason_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_mason_infix}" || true

      local z_mason_code
      z_mason_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_mason_infix}") || z_mason_code=""
      if test "${z_mason_code}" = "200"; then
        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}"
      else
        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN}"
      fi
    else
      buc_log_args "Skipping moniker ${z_moniker} with unrecognized lifecycle state: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi

    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" "${z_state}"

    z_index=$((z_index + 1))
  done
}

content.text: Now reshape `rbgp_depot_list` to call the helper and walk the emitted fact files for stdout rendering.

input.old_string:
rbgp_depot_list() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "List all depot instances and their status"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step 'Authenticate as Payor'
  test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID is not set"
  test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}"  || buc_die "RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID is not set"
  
  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor via OAuth"

  buc_step 'Query depot projects'
  local -r z_filter="projectId:${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-* AND lifecycleState:ACTIVE"
  local -r z_list_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects?filter=${z_filter// /%20}"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_list_projects"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List depot projects" "depot_list_projects"
  
  local z_project_count
  z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_list_projects" '.projects // [] | length') || z_project_count=0

  if test "${z_project_count}" -eq 0; then
    buc_info "No active depot projects found"
    return 0
  fi

  buc_step "Validating ${z_project_count} depot(s)"
  
  local z_depot_index=0
  local z_complete_count=0
  local z_broken_count=0
  
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "=== DEPOT SUMMARY ==="
  printf "%-40s %s\n" "PROJECT_ID" "STATUS"

  while test "${z_depot_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
    local z_project_id
    z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_list_projects" ".projects[${z_depot_index}].projectId") || continue

    # Strip this installation's depot prefix (CLOUD_PREFIX + 'd-') to recover
    # the moniker; projects outside the namespace are listed but skipped here
    # since their moniker can't be inferred from the new naming scheme.
    local z_depot_name=""
    if [[ "${z_project_id}" == "${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"* ]]; then
      z_depot_name="${z_project_id#"${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"}"
    fi

    local z_status="CHECKING"
    local z_mason_expected="${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-${z_depot_name}"

    local z_mason_email
    z_mason_email=$(rbgu_sa_email_capture "${z_mason_expected}" "${z_project_id}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to compose Mason email for depot ${z_depot_name}"
    local z_mason_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_email}"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_mason_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_list_mason_${z_depot_index}" || true

    local z_mason_code
    z_mason_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_list_mason_${z_depot_index}") || z_mason_code=""
    if test "${z_mason_code}" = "200"; then
      z_status="COMPLETE"
      z_complete_count=$((z_complete_count + 1))
    else
      z_status="BROKEN"
      z_broken_count=$((z_broken_count + 1))
    fi

    printf "%-40s %s\n" "${z_project_id}" "${z_status}"

    z_depot_index=$((z_depot_index + 1))
  done
  
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "=== SUMMARY ==="
  buc_info "Total depots: ${z_project_count}"
  buc_info "Complete: ${z_complete_count}"
  buc_info "Broken: ${z_broken_count}"
  
  if test "${z_broken_count}" -gt 0; then
    buc_info ""
    buc_info "Note: BROKEN depots may have missing resources (Mason SA, repository, or bucket)"
    buc_info "Consider investigating broken depots or destroying them if no longer needed"
  fi
}
input.new_string:
rbgp_depot_list() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "List all depot instances and their status"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step 'Authenticate as Payor'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor via OAuth"

  buc_step 'Probe depot states and emit per-moniker fact files'
  zrbgp_depot_state_emit "${z_token}"

  buc_step 'Render depot summary'
  local -r z_prefix_match="${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-"
  local z_total_count=0
  local z_complete_count=0
  local z_broken_count=0
  local z_delete_requested_count=0

  buc_info ""
  buc_info "=== DEPOT SUMMARY ==="
  printf "%-40s %s\n" "PROJECT_ID" "STATUS"

  # Walk emitted fact files; filename stem is the moniker, content is the state.
  shopt -s nullglob
  local z_fact_path
  for z_fact_path in "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"/*.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}; do
    local z_basename="${z_fact_path##*/}"
    local z_moniker="${z_basename%.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}}"
    local z_project_id="${z_prefix_match}${z_moniker}"
    local z_state
    z_state=$(<"${z_fact_path}")
    test -n "${z_state}" || buc_die "Empty state in fact file: ${z_fact_path}"

    printf "%-40s %s\n" "${z_project_id}" "${z_state}"
    z_total_count=$((z_total_count + 1))
    case "${z_state}" in
      "${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}")          z_complete_count=$((z_complete_count + 1)) ;;
      "${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN}")            z_broken_count=$((z_broken_count + 1)) ;;
      "${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}")  z_delete_requested_count=$((z_delete_requested_count + 1)) ;;
    esac
  done
  shopt -u nullglob

  if test "${z_total_count}" -eq 0; then
    buc_info "No depot projects found"
    return 0
  fi

  buc_info ""
  buc_info "=== SUMMARY ==="
  buc_info "Total depots:     ${z_total_count}"
  buc_info "Complete:         ${z_complete_count}"
  buc_info "Broken:           ${z_broken_count}"
  buc_info "Delete-requested: ${z_delete_requested_count}"

  if test "${z_broken_count}" -gt 0; then
    buc_info ""
    buc_info "Note: BROKEN depots may have missing resources (Mason SA, repository, or bucket)"
    buc_info "Consider investigating broken depots or destroying them if no longer needed"
  fi
}
